claim: Archon is an operating system for AI coding that acts as both a UI for humans (project/knowledge management) and an MCP server for AI assistants to share context
actionable: Use Archon as your command center: manage tasks, knowledge, and documents in its UI while connecting it to Claude Code or other MCP-supporting assistants via the MCP server for seamless context sharing
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Claude (LLMs in general) tends to overengineer solutions by default—when you ask for a simple app, it may generate 100+ tasks instead of 15-20
actionable: Explicitly tell your coding assistant in global rules and prompts to keep things simple and avoid overengineering; be specific about task count limits (e.g., 'no more than 20 tasks')
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: The BMAD method (Business/Agentic/Managed/Development) is a context engineering strategy that uses sub-agents (analyst, PM, architect, scrum master) for planning and development workflows
actionable: For new projects, start with the analyst agent to research and create a project brief, then use the PM to create a PRD, architect to define tasks, and development agents to implement
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Archon performs RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) by crawling documentation (e.g., Claude Code SDK, Next.js docs) and allowing agents to search through this knowledge base during planning
actionable: Crawl relevant documentation into Archon's knowledge base, then reference it in agent commands like 'search how to leverage the Cloud Code SDK' to get research grounded in actual docs rather than hallucinations
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Sub-agents in BMAD (and Claude Code) are implemented as prompted markdown files, not built-in agent features—any coding assistant can use them by reading instructions from a file
actionable: Create markdown files for each agent persona (analyst, PM, architect) with activation notices, roles, principles, and available commands; reference these files to switch agent contexts
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Green-field (new) vs. brownfield (existing codebase) projects require different BMAD workflows: green-field focuses on external docs/research, brownfield focuses on existing code patterns
actionable: For new projects, have agents research external documentation heavily; for existing projects, have agents reference the codebase structure and patterns instead
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: PRP (Prompt Response Pattern) framework complements BMAD: after BMAD planning creates a PRD, use PRP to turn it into structured prompts with validation gates, pseudo-code, and anti-patterns
actionable: After BMAD generates your PRD, create a PRP document with purpose, core principles, documentation requirements, desired codebase structure, validation loops, and anti-patterns, then execute it in Claude
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Archon stores documents in Superbase (cloud database), allowing multiple users to share the same Archon instance by running local instances pointing to shared cloud Superbase
actionable: Set up Superbase in the cloud (superbase.com) and configure Archon with shared credentials; team members run local Archon instances but all read/write to the same cloud database
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Archon requires Docker, Superbase account (free tier works), and API keys for OpenAI/Gemini; setup is quick (clone repo, set env vars, run docker-compose, migrate DB)
actionable: Install Docker Desktop, create a free Superbase account, get OpenAI API key, clone Archon repo, set environment variables (service role key, data URL), run migrations, and spin up containers
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: When setting up Archon with Superbase, use the service role key (not the anonymous key) for environment variables
actionable: In Superbase project settings > API section, reveal and copy the service role key, not the anonymous key, for your Archon environment variables
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Connect Archon as an MCP server to Claude Code with a single command: 'claude mcp install archon' (once Archon is running locally)
actionable: Ensure Archon is running locally (docker compose), then run 'claude mcp install archon' in terminal, and verify with 'claude mcp list'
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Archon integrates sub-agents with tasks: different agents can be assigned to different task categories, creating clear boundaries for who implements what
actionable: Label documents and tasks by agent name (e.g., 'Created by: Mary') and assign tasks to specific agents so they coordinate with clear ownership boundaries
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Web search (built into most LLMs) and RAG (via Archon) are complementary, not competing—agents can use both for more complete research
actionable: Let your agents perform both web searches and Archon RAG queries; they will naturally combine results for better context
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Archon uses WebSockets for real-time UI updates—documents and tasks created by agents appear immediately in the UI without refresh
actionable: Rely on real-time synchronization between Claude Code (via MCP) and Archon UI; no manual refresh needed when agents modify projects
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Validation/code review are essential with AI coding—even structured approaches like BMAD won't avoid hallucinations, so always inspect agent outputs
actionable: After agents generate PRDs, tasks, or code, manually review for errors, contradictions, and hallucinations (e.g., unwanted Python wrappers in a JavaScript project) before proceeding
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Context engineering strategies (BMAD, PRP, Spectrum of Development) are composable and work together—use BMAD for planning, PRP for execution
actionable: Combine BMAD's planning workflow with PRP's execution structure: BMAD generates PRDs and tasks, then use PRP to turn the PRD into a structured prompt with validation gates
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: LLM inconsistency with tools: agents don't always use MCP (or other tools) consistently—be explicit in prompts about which tools to use and when
actionable: Add explicit instructions to global rules telling agents 'use Archon for X', 'search Archon docs before web search', etc.; don't assume they'll leverage tools correctly
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!

claim: Start simple and iterate: even with Archon and structured approaches, expecting perfect output from one shot is unrealistic—plan for multiple refinement cycles
actionable: After initial generation, expect to correct hallucinations, refine specs, and iterate; build with multiple feedback loops rather than trying to one-shot the entire app
source_title: Archon Beta Launch Livestream - What You Missed!
